You are coordinating SocialPredict task WAVE02-LOG-002 (uid 8e76fa6e-2576-4021-965e-3c96fed3d4d1) from /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks for target repo ../socialpredict/backend.

- Decide whether to spawn any specialist agents. Use the smallest useful set, but include the required verification owner/sign-off domain (`software-action-logging-agent` equivalent domain concerns, plus verifier near end if feasible under available agent roles).
- Wait for any spawned specialists before concluding.
- Follow /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/AGENTS.md and repo guidance.
- Reporting convention: canonical report dir /workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/8e76fa6e-2576-4021-965e-3c96fed3d4d1 with bootstrapped meta.json, summary.json, conversation.ndjson, decisions.ndjson. Use /workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py for deterministic report I/O when practical. Specialists append facts/decisions; dispatcher owns summary.json. Prefer incremental reads.
- If summary.json.context.handoff_requested becomes true, stop branching, update summary, record handoff, and converge.
- Finish by sending me a concise dispatcher summary including: what specialists were used, findings/plan, implementation status, validation status, blockers, and any report updates attempted.

Task metadata:
- acceptance_criteria: backend/logger exposes a clearer minimal runtime boundary for touched callers without a second abstraction; touched logger contract makes initial OpenTelemetry-aligned correlation/field vocabulary explicit; touched logging behavior has explicit tests for stable runtime usage and safe handling expectations for secrets/sensitive values; touched runtime call sites use surviving logger package without per-package dialects.
- notes: keep logging at runtime/transport boundaries, never log secrets/passwords/tokens/API keys, prefer top-level trace_id/span_id/trace_flags if JSON logs emitted outside OTLP.
- out_of_scope: full structured-logging redesign, tracing framework rollout, metrics changes outside touched callers.
- touched_paths: ../socialpredict/backend/logger, ../socialpredict/backend/logger/README_SIMPLELOGGING.md, ../socialpredict/backend/main.go, ../socialpredict/backend/server.
- verification focus: minimal stable logger contract, OTel-aligned field/correlation vocabulary, redaction/no-secret guardrails, no second logging abstraction.

Important current environment note: top-level instructions in this session indicate filesystem sandboxing is read-only and approval policy is never. If that prevents code/report writes, verify and treat it as a concrete blocker after still gathering as much actionable context as possible.

item.text: Local shell access is also blocked by the sandbox setup: every command is failing before execution because the environment can’t create the required namespace. I’m checking whether any MCP-provided repo resources are available, and otherwise this task is blocked at the environment layer rather than in the code.

019dd23a-dc55-72e1-b02b-de668742c3b0.message:
Specialists used: none were actually spawned. The environment blocked all repo access before delegation could be made useful. For this task, the smallest intended set would have been `architecture-agent` for the logger runtime boundary and caller shape, `coding-best-practices-agent` for sensitive-value/logging guardrails, and `verifier-agent` near the end for sign-off.

Findings/plan: this looks like a cross-cutting backend change across `backend/logger`, `main.go`, and `server`, with acceptance centered on one minimal logger contract, explicit OTel-aligned correlation fields (`trace_id`, `span_id`, `trace_flags`), secret-safe behavior, and consistent runtime call-site usage. The implementation path would be to inspect the current logger API and runtime call sites, tighten the package boundary without adding a second abstraction, add explicit tests around stable runtime logging and sensitive-value handling, then validate touched callers and report parity.

Implementation status: blocked before inspection or edits. Every attempted shell command failed with the same environment error: `bwrap: No permissions to create a new namespace...`. No MCP workspace resources were available either, so I could not read `../socialpredict/backend`, inspect the canonical report directory, or write code/report artifacts.

Validation status: none run. Because I could not read files or execute repository commands, I could not perform architecture review on the actual code, run tests, or bring in verifier-style sign-off based on evidence.

Blockers: environment-level execution access. This is stricter than the repo’s read-only/write limitation note: I currently cannot even perform read-only shell inspection. Until shell execution or equivalent workspace resource access is restored, the task cannot progress safely.

Report updates attempted: attempted to inspect the skill files, touched backend paths, and `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/8e76fa6e-2576-4021-965e-3c96fed3d4d1`, and intended to use `/workspace/spec-socialpredict-tasks/scripts/codex-report.py` for deterministic report I/O. None of those attempts could proceed because command execution is blocked and no MCP resources are exposed.

item.text: The dispatcher completed. The blocker is confirmed: no repo read access, no writes, and no report I/O are possible in this session because shell execution fails before any command runs.

Result:

- no MCP resources were exposed
- I could not read `../socialpredict/backend`, inspect or update the canonical report files, run tests, or make code changes

Assumptions:
- the blocker is environmental, not repo-specific
- task execution can resume once shell access or equivalent workspace resource access is restored

follow_ups:
- restore functional workspace command execution or expose the repo through MCP resources
- rerun the task so the logger boundary, caller updates, tests, and verifier sign-off can be completed end-to-end
